Question: Use the definition of the definite integral to evaluate $$\int_{0}^{3} ( 2 x - 1 ) d x .$$ Use a right-endpoint approximation to generate the Riemann sum.
Use the formula for the area of a trapezoid to evaluate $$\int_{2}^{4} ( 2 x + 3 ) d x .$$
Find the net signed area of $$f ( x ) = x - 2$$ over the interval $$[ 0 , 6 ] ,$$ illustrated in the following image.
Find the total area between the function $$f ( x ) = 2 x$$ and the x -axis over the interval $$[ -3 , 3 ] .$$
Use the properties of the definite integral to express the definite integral of $$f ( x ) = 6 x^{3} - 4 x^{2} + 2 x - 3$$ over the interval $$[ 1 , 3 ]$$ as the sum of four definite integrals.
If it is known that $$\int_{1}^{5} f ( x ) d x = -3$$ and $$\int_{2}^{5} f ( x ) d x = 4 ,$$ find the value of $$\int_{1}^{2} f ( x ) d x .$$
Find the average value of $$f ( x ) = 6 - 2 x$$ over the interval $$[ 0 , 3 ] .$$
